features.summary: Delta reported Q1 2022 results with $8.2 billion revenue (79% of 2019 levels) and $200 million free cash flow. March marked an inflection point with 10% operating margin and first positive unit revenue versus 2019 in two years. The company saw strong demand recovery from Presidents Day onward as Omicron receded, with March being Delta's best cash sales month in history despite 10% fewer seats than 2019. Business travel accelerated with corporate fares turning positive versus 2019 for the first time, and domestic corporate sales reached 70% recovery.
features.management_outlook: Management expects Q2 operating margins of 12-14% with revenue recovery reaching 93-97% of 2019 levels on 84% capacity restoration. They anticipate strong summer demand driven by pent-up travel demand, consumer shift from goods to services, and lifting travel restrictions. The company remains confident in achieving meaningful full-year 2022 profit and expects to exceed 2019 financial performance by 2024. Management emphasized maintaining operational reliability and staying nimble on capacity decisions while prioritizing sustained profitability.
features.risks: Key risks include macro uncertainty affecting second-half capacity plans, elevated fuel prices averaging $3.20-$3.35 per gallon in Q2 (50% higher than 2019), and ongoing supply chain challenges. The company faces potential demand softening concerns, though not currently visible in bookings. International recovery remains dependent on border reopenings, particularly China and Japan, while pre-departure testing requirements continue to constrain transatlantic demand. Labor supply constraints across the industry and operational challenges during peak travel periods pose additional risks.
features.key_drivers: Premium products and business travel recovery are driving revenue growth, with premium products leading the recovery and domestic premium revenue reaching 100% of 2019 levels. American Express remuneration hit a record $1.2 billion (25% above 2019), keeping the company on track for $5+ billion annually. Cargo revenue reached record levels, up 51% versus 2019. The company's disciplined capacity approach, brand strength, and operational investments during the pandemic are creating competitive advantages and supporting pricing power in the strong demand environment.
